name: Modern Chess
author: Gabriel Vicente Maura (1968) / AGP port
description: Modern Chess (Ajedrez Moderno, Gabriel Vicente Maura, Puerto Rico, 1968), on a 9x9 board. Each side has the full standard army plus one extra Prime Minister (a compound bishop+knight piece, like Capablanca's Archbishop) and a ninth pawn. The back rank is R N B M K Q B N R, with the King on the centre file (e), the Queen to its right and the Prime Minister to its left, and nine pawns on the 2nd / 8th ranks. Pawns move and capture as in standard chess (double-step, en passant) and promote to Q/R/B/N/M on the last rank. The king castles by sliding two squares toward either rook. Checkmate wins; stalemate and the usual fifty-move / repetition / insufficient-material situations draw.
